scenario: A TypeScript Job Worker handles validate-vat Service Tasks. The handler must occasionally fail the job with a specific BPMN error code (e.g., INVALID_VAT) -- to trigger an Error Boundary Event. Which @camunda8/sdk API call fits?
question: Which @camunda8/sdk API call fits?

options.text: ) -- the generic job failure method in the @camunda8/sdk TypeScript SDK. The fail method decrements the job retry count and, when retries reach zero, creates a Zeebe Incident visible in Operate. It does not propagate a BPMN error code to the process model, so an Error Boundary Event configured with errorCode = "INVALID_VAT" will not be triggered by calling fail.

"}) -- the @camunda8/sdk TypeScript SDK's explicit BPMN error method. This completes the job with a BPMN error that Zeebe propagates to the nearest matching Error Boundary Event or Error Event Subprocess subscribed to the INVALID_VAT error code. The worker signals a business error condition through the BPMN process model rather than a technical failure.

Throw a JavaScript Error object and rely on the SDK to automatically convert it into a BPMN error with the required error code. Throwing an uncaught JS Error in the handler is treated as a job failure, not a BPMN error. error() method must be called explicitly with the appropriate errorCode to trigger Error Boundary Events.

Return a special magic-field object {__error: "INVALID_VAT"} from the job handler function as a convention for signalling BPMN errors. The @camunda8/sdk has no magic-field convention for error signalling through return values. error({errorCode, errorMessage}) to propagate a BPMN error; there is no implicit detection of error-intent from returned objects or any special field names on returned values.

a.text: Incorrect. Per apis-tools/typescript/api-reference/index.md:489, the @camunda8/sdk job worker handle exposes job.error({ errorCode, errorMessage? }) -- explicitly described as "throws a business error". The runtime propagates the errorCode to the nearest matching Error Catch (Boundary or Event Subprocess) per BPMN error propagation semantics.

b.text: Correct. Per apis-tools/typescript/api-reference/index.md:487, job.fail({ errorMessage, retries?, retryBackoff? }) decrements retries and (when retries reach 0) creates an Incident; it does NOT propagate a BPMN error code.

c.text: Incorrect. JS Error converts to failure, not BPMN error with errorCode.

explanation: Per apis-tools/typescript/api-reference/index.md:487, job.fail({ errorMessage, retries?, retryBackoff? }) decrements retries and (when retries reach 0) creates an Incident; it does NOT propagate a BPMN error code.
